channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abandoned hire bikes, 6th March
item.description:
Abandoned hire bikes overcrowded parked in the middle of the little Newport Street near Hippodrome Casino

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Lisle Street, Chinatown, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple potholes on the carriage way, 6th March
item.description:
Massive potholes on the road which cause residents cars to be damaged
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 94, Bromley Hill, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep pothole in road on the railway bridge, 6th March
item.description:
It is on the carriageway going towards Erith. Very deep and certainly a danger to cyclists/motor cyclists and cars. Danger of damage to wheels/tyres. It has been there for some weeks and does not have any spray paint round it to say it has been previously reported.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Erith Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal flytipping, 6th March
item.description:
There is a broken armchair and two pallets placed outside number 23/25. These were in the front garden, but have now worked their way on the pavement, and are a hazard for people frequenting, who have disabilities. This needs to be removed immediately.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Linthorpe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The park has multiple things that need to be fixed it use to be a lovely park but now its full of gaulty stuff and many hazards that a child could really get hurt, 6th March
item.description:
• Exposed Nails and Sharp Fixings: There are large metal nails and bolts protruding from the rotting wooden walkways and stairs. This is an immediate puncture risk for children using the equipment. • Structural Rot & Missing Panels: The wooden platforms are crumbling, with holes and missing planks. • Missing Climbing Equipment: The climbing rope near the ground was pulled out months ago due to age but was never replaced, leaving the equipment incomplete and unsafe. • Failed Safety Surfacing: The "wet pour" flooring is bubbling and torn, creating significant trip hazards. • Abandoned Equipment: The zip line and spinning toy have been out of action for up to 2 years. The temporary fencing around them is often falling over and is not a permanent safety solution. I am aware that a refurbishment is planned, but as the weather gets warmer and the park gets busier, these exposed nails and rotten structures pose an immediate danger. I am requesting an urgent safety inspection and a clear timeline for when these hazards will be removed or made safe. I look forward to your response. Regards

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Knatchbull Road, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Commercial bins overflowing, 6th March
item.description:
This is a recurrent problem causing a public health hazard and needs attention as it is encouraging others to dump rubbish there.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eden Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Assortment of general flytipped waste, 5th March
item.description:
A pile of general goods has been dumped by the side of the building, by the corner of Rollins Street and Lovelinch Close.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rollins Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rollins Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London

item.title: Parks on the corner of Felton Lea every night, 5th March
item.description:
Doesn't park in accordance with junction restrictions. Sits in car talking disturbing residents late at night. Car alarm is noisy when set and unset often Manu times a night.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Felton Lea, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: David miller 3.3.26 Road markings fading, Borough f/up req, 5th March
item.description:
David miller 3.3.26 Road markings fading, Borough f/up req HIGH STREET SIDCUP - STATION ROAD - ELM ROAD - MAIN ROAD
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Station Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large quickly degrading pothole, 5th March
item.description:
This pothole surrounds a drain which bangs continuously due to the excessive traffic and as there are numerous quarry trucks driving over it everyday its degrading quickly and will collapse soon. Also the noise is very disturbing to the families and elderly and everyone that live here. Please sort it out ASAP.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, North Cray Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light was fixed and is now out again !, 5th March
item.description:
This light was fixed after I reported it but is now out again!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milton Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
